business_ideas.idea: Blockworks: crypto media platform (podcasts, newsletters, reporters, conferences)
Blockworks Research: subscription analytics on crypto protocols (financials, governance, metrics)
Better event ticketing platform for conferences (10x better than Eventbrite)
Hair transplant clinic/platform (consolidate shady market into trusted brand)

predictions.text: Hair transplant market grows from $5B to $30B by 2031 (20% CAGR), consolidates like Lasik did
Crypto is best risk/reward allocation for next decade despite traditional markets remaining stable

disagreements: Sam skeptical of concentrated crypto portfolio; Guest defends high-conviction bet. Sam reads history as cyclical (long-term trends persist), Guest sees potential systemic breakdown. Sam thinks 90% of crypto/web3 is scam, Guest agrees but says remaining builders are serious.
dynamics: Guest is intellectually curious, self-aware, shares personal stories transparently (MLM energy drink scheme). Both hosts push back on guest's 100% crypto bet; guest defends with philosophy of regret minimization. Collaborative tone; Sam/Shaan respect guest's conviction even when disagreeing.
frameworks: Reverse-engineer markets: study successful businesses (Bloomberg, Politico Pro), build 10x better version for niche
Event consolidation wins: fewer, bigger conferences > many small ones (sponsorship budgets split vs consolidate)
Barbell risk strategy: safe liquid assets + high-risk private company equity, not double-high-risk
First-time founders iterate, not strategize: grand vision emerges after months of trying things
Multi-level marketing taught founder about grit, sales, and confidence despite ethical questions
learnings: Media businesses work backwards from monetization: monetize first (events/sponsorships), then add content layers (podcasts, research)
Crypto media has 10x better monetization than general interest media; research subscriptions outperform ads in downturns
Founding a company forces personal growth in ways employment cannot; fulfillment matters more than billionaire status
Conferences in cyclical markets are risky: must book venues 4-5 years out but can't predict market cycles (CoinDesk example)
Crypto attracts both real builders and hucksters; the industry's ideological strength attracts scammers but also genuine innovation

banger_quotes.quote: No one ever got wealthy through diversification
I want to only do things I want and things I have to do, not things I should do
First-time founders don't have grand vision, just iterate day after day
You can't have innovation without fraud; America's optimism creates both

numbers_dropped: Blockworks 2023 revenue: ~$25M (guest wouldn't confirm exact but said exceeded $20M goal)
Blockworks 2023 EBITDA: ~$8M (guest bootstrapped/profitable since day one)
Series A (April/May 2023): raised $12M at $135M valuation from 10T Holdings, Framework Ventures, and Santiago
Permissionless conference revenue: over $10M last year
Hair transplant market: $5B now, projected $30B by 2031 (20% CAGR)
Hoppin founder secondary proceeds: $100M+ despite startup collapse
Politico Pro (research platform): ~$100M revenue before $1B sale
notable: Guest very transparent about past involvement in MLM scheme (energy drink), uses as origin story for entrepreneurial confidence. Defends crypto conviction with regret minimization logic. Sean (co-host) ghosted guest via text before episode—guest called out on air humorously.
